I am a third-year Electronics and Communication Engineering student at a tier-2 engineering college in Tamil Nadu, India. I did not start with clarity about what I wanted to do - I chose ECE because I was curious about how things worked at the hardware level, and that curiosity has only grown.

Most of my peers went toward software and web development. I kept getting pulled toward the other direction - toward the layer where code becomes electricity, where abstractions end and physics begins.

This is not a portfolio in the traditional sense. I built it because I noticed that most engineering portfolios only show the finished product. I wanted to document the failed simulations, the misread datasheets, and the bugs that took three days to find.
Right now I am a student who is genuinely learning. I want to work in digital design, RTL development, or chip design. I am building toward that, one project and one mistake at a time.
